mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent recession risk, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate positive 60-day returns in > 50% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases by > 1% but SPX forward 20-day returns remain negative in > 65% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 200.0, inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 200.0 coincides with positive 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending forecasts and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings growth, directly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates negatively with 60-day SPX returns or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following a > 0.5% INDPRO decline.
